hero.headline: Employer expansion and logistics depth keep Columbus interesting for patient rental buyers.
hero.note: TELAJ likes this market for practical buy-and-hold and small multifamily, not for hype or instant flips.

markets.summary: Data center, logistics, and university-driven demand continue to support rental depth.
Research, health care, and employer growth keep demand solid, but pricing is less forgiving.
Still affordable and usable for yield seekers, but exitability is slower and neighborhood selection matters more.
Still fundamentally attractive long term, but current supply pressure and basis make patience more important.

newsSignals.headline: Large cloud and data infrastructure expansion announced near the metro logistics corridor
newsSignals.implication: higher long-term housing demand from contractors, operations staff, and follow-on employers

newsSignals.headline: Major life-science and hospital hiring wave expands local high-income renter base
newsSignals.implication: supportive for stable rental demand, especially near employment clusters

newsSignals.headline: New permit volume and multifamily deliveries continue to rise
newsSignals.implication: future rent competition and slower rent growth if absorption cools

deals.address: Triplex near clinic corridor, Cleveland, OH

items: Depreciation can shelter some rental income, but the mechanics depend on basis allocation and your full tax picture.
Mortgage interest matters, but it does not turn a weak property into a strong deal.
Cost segregation is an advanced strategy worth discussing only with a qualified CPA.
Entity structure, liability protection, and transfer issues should be reviewed with a real attorney and CPA.
